title: plumaria alpina
intro: Plumaria alpina, also known as Plume-grass or Sea-plume, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family of Gracilariales. It is a marine red algae that is commonly found in the intertidal zone and has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Plumaria alpina is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory problems, as well as to promote immune function and overall well-being.
